45.reason: [The variable 'n' is used uninitialized in the first iteration of the loop. The 'do-while' loop reads 'n' only at the end of each iteration, so the first check of 'n' (e.g., if(n==1)) uses an indeterminate value, leading to undefined behavior. This can cause incorrect operation interpretation or crashes. Additionally, the condition in push() should be 'top < max - 1' or 'top != max - 1' because valid indices are 0 to max-1; current condition 'top != max' allows top to become max, causing buffer overflow. Also, when pop returns -1 to indicate error, if the actual popped value was -1 (a valid integer), it would be mistaken for an error, leading to missing output.]
